title: Prevent run-tasks test-gate CPU blowup
summary: Running the run-tasks skill pinned CPU at 800% for ~5 minutes. Root cause: the validation gate spawned the test-runner with the full resolved test command (vitest run, full suite) with no file scoping and no worker cap, so vitest's default worker pool saturated all ~8 cores. It was compounded by no run timeout, a background-plus-busy-wait (ps grep) polling loop that made it feel hung, an npm install mid-run, and npx. CI already runs the full suite capped at --maxWorkers=2, so the local gate was both redundant and unbounded. Decision: make the local gate run affected tests only (CI stays the full-suite backstop), add an always-on worker cap, a hard timeout with process-group kill, serialize test-runners across workspaces, fix execution hygiene, and make the runner report an explicit status enum.
decisions: Local run-tasks gate runs affected tests only; CI (CircleCI yarn test --maxWorkers=2, full suite) remains the full-suite regression backstop. A full-suite precommit hook is optional/later.
Affected selection uses an explicit list: vitest related <touched-source-files> plus touched test files run directly. Not git --changed (avoids the untracked-new-test-file hole mid-TDD).
When 0 affected tests are selected, the gate WARN-passes (never silent, never blocks). No file-type tiering between source and docs/config changes.
Always-on worker cap of --maxWorkers=75% on every gate test run (leaves headroom on an 8-core box, no full lockup).

detect_tooling.py owns a framework-aware test_affected template with a {files} placeholder and the worker cap baked in. run-tasks substitutes the touched files; test-runner runs the command verbatim and stays a dumb executor.

Cross-framework: vitest uses related (two-segment: direct test files + related source dependents); jest uses --findRelatedTests; pytest has no native affected selection so detect_tooling.py emits a null template and run-tasks falls back to a capped full suite plus WARN. No pytest-testmon investment now.
Hard 180s wall-clock timeout on the test run. On timeout, kill the whole process group (reap vitest workers, not just the parent) and treat it as a gate failure.

Serialize test-runners across workspaces (one vitest process at a time). Lint-runners stay parallel. Parallelism lives inside vitest's bounded worker pool, never across per-file or per-workspace agents.

Execution hygiene in test-runner: run the command in the foreground and block on it (no background, no ps-grep busy-wait), use the resolved local binary not npx, and fail fast if deps are missing rather than auto-installing.

runner-result-contract carries an explicit status enum {pass|fail|warn|timeout|deps-missing} alongside counts. run-tasks branches on status: pass/warn proceed (warn prints a coverage-deferred message), fail/timeout/deps-missing block.
next_action: Run /to-tasks to break this into implementation tasks across detect_tooling.py, test-runner.md, run-tasks/SKILL.md, and the runner-result-contract.
problem_statement: The run-tasks validation gate spawns the test-runner agent with the workspace's full resolved test command (vitest run --reporter=json) with no file scoping and no concurrency cap. vitest's default worker pool is sized to the CPU count, so a single full-suite run saturated all ~8 logical cores, pinning the machine at 800% for ~5 minutes. There was also no run timeout, a background-then-busy-wait polling loop, an npm install side effect, and npx usage that together made the run feel hung and unbounded.
solution: Stop running the full suite locally. The gate runs only the tests affected by the touched files (vitest related + direct touched test files), bounded by an always-on --maxWorkers=75% cap and a 180s process-group-killed timeout. CI remains the full-suite backstop. Framework-aware affected-command templates live in detect_tooling.py; run-tasks fills in touched files and serializes test-runners across workspaces; test-runner runs verbatim in the foreground, fails fast on missing deps, and reports an explicit status enum the gate branches on.
evidence: Observed: run-tasks pinned CPU at 800% for ~5 minutes on Quaestor-Web. Confirmed in code: run-tasks/SKILL.md:135-137 spawns test-runner with the full resolved command; test-runner.md:59,193 runs vitest run --reporter=json with no cap; vitest.config.ts:16 sets pool: threads with no worker cap; .circleci/continue-config.yml:1058 runs the full suite at --maxWorkers=2.
success_metrics: A single-file change runs only that file's affected tests locally, not the whole suite
Local gate test runs never exceed 75% of cores (no full machine lockup)
No gate test run exceeds 180s; hangs are killed at the process-group level and reported as failures
0-affected and timeout and deps-missing outcomes are distinguishable from pass/fail via an explicit status field
implementation_decisions: detect_tooling.py: add per-framework test_affected template resolution (vitest/jest real templates, pytest null), with --maxWorkers=75% baked into the template and a {files} placeholder

agents/test-runner.md: accept a touched-files input and substitute {files}; run foreground/blocking; use resolved local binary not npx; fail fast on missing deps; wrap the run in a portable 180s timeout (gtimeout or portable fallback, since macOS has no native timeout) that kills the process group; emit explicit status

skills/run-tasks/SKILL.md: pass the per-workspace touched-file list to the test-runner; serialize test-runners across workspaces while keeping lint-runners parallel; branch the gate on the status enum (warn proceeds with a message, fail/timeout/deps-missing block)
contracts/runner-result-contract.md and runner-result-schema.json: add a status enum {pass|fail|warn|timeout|deps-missing} alongside the existing {passed,failed,skipped} counts

out_of_scope.rationale: No investment in pytest testmon at this stage.
Deferred; optional for a later effort.

out_of_scope.text: Per-N dividing of the worker cap across parallel workspace runners
out_of_scope.rationale: Rejected in favor of serializing runners.

risks_and_tradeoffs: Affected selection can miss non-static coupling (dynamic imports, shared global state, snapshot/fixture files, integration tests that do not statically import the changed file); CI's full suite catches these before merge so a local miss surfaces in CI, not prod
75% cap makes large affected sets slower than uncapped, accepted because runs are short-lived and machine responsiveness matters more
180s timeout could false-trigger on a genuinely huge affected set (shared-util change); treated as an acceptable signal that the blast radius is large
macOS lacks a native timeout binary, so the process-group-kill wrapper needs a portable implementation (gtimeout or a perl/background-pid fallback)
further_notes: runner-result-contract and runner-result-schema were recently moved from claude-code-shared/resources/ to claude-code-shared/contracts/; edit the contracts/ copies
detect_workspaces (detect_tooling.py:28-46) defines a workspace as a dir with package.json or pyproject.toml at repo root or one level deep; a workspace is a project, not a file
